title: North Telangana braces for 45°C heatwave; storms likely in south - Hyderabad Mail
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Large parts of north Telangana are likely to remain under intense heatwave conditions, with temperatures expected to touch 44°C to 45°C in several districts over the next 24 hours.
Adilabad, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Mancherial, Karimnagar, Rajanna Sircilla, Kamareddy, Jayashankar Bhupalpally and Mulugu are expected to record the highest daytime temperatures.
Weather officials have also forecast isolated intense thunderstorms in parts of southern Telangana, including Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Jogulamba Gadwal, Narayanpet, Vikarabad and Mahabubnagar districts. Thunderstorm activity is likely from afternoon till late night, accompanied by gusty winds in isolated places.
Hyderabad is expected to witness largely dry weather conditions, though isolated light rain activity may occur in some parts of the city during evening hours.
The sharp rise in temperatures has increased discomfort levels across Telangana, with residents advised to avoid prolonged outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours and stay hydrated.
